confidence_rationale: Video consists entirely of presentation slides from a conference (SAGES 2021 'Reimagining a Healthier World') describing a virtual laparoscopic surgery course implemented in Bolivia. No actual FLS task performance is visible. Slides show course structure, participant demographics, evaluation results, and conclusions — all educational/didactic content with no trainee performing peg transfer.
cannot_determine: No task performance visible in any frame
No peg transfer equipment, pegboard, or Maryland dissectors shown

teaching_content.skill_level_demonstrated: N/A — presentation only, no skill demonstration
teaching_content.course_summary: Virtual Basic Laparoscopic Surgery Course delivered Fall 2020 in Santa Cruz de la Sierra, Bolivia. Two synchronous cohorts (July-September and November-December 2020) via Zoom. Content included didactics (pneumoperitoneum physiology, electrosurgery, access, equipment, risks/benefits) and skills training (object transfer, precision cutting, intra- and extracorporeal knot tying). Evaluation via pre/post multiple-choice tests and video submission of skills. Participants: 24 total (8 hospitals represented, 63% male, 50% resident/50% attending). Post-course skill confidence improved significantly in object transfer and precision cutting. Instructors included Bolivian and US-based faculty.

scoring_decision: No FLS task1 performance to score. This is an educational presentation about a laparoscopic surgery training program, not a recorded performance of the peg transfer task. Return minimal assessment per protocol for unusable/instructional content.
